metadata.title:
SPOKANE AGENT
FOR G. N. NAMED


AS A REeATER

pages.raw_text:
Under the ruling of the U. S. su-
preme court yesterday in uphold
ing the Elkins act the Great North
ern Is compelled to pay total fine
of $4,000 for rebating. Among the
individual officials indicted is D. G.
Black, general agent of the COM
pany in Spokane. The charge
brought was that the company,
through it's agents, had granted re-
bates on oats and corn shipped by
the Devereaux company from Min
neapolis to Washington state
points. The company defended the
rebates on the theory that the FL
kins law had been repealed by the
Hepburn act, which last made a
provision for punishment as i'm
prisonment The Elkins law, un-
der which the Great Northern was
prosecuted, fixes only fines, and the
railroad attorneys depended on the
plea that the case had been prose
cuted under a law that had been
displaced by a new one. The SUI
preme court held the contrary view
and what the supreme court hap.
pens to think about stands.


AS the company pays all the ex-
pense and there is no danger of i'm
prisonment, Mr.. Black is naturally
not worrying. He says he has not
given the matter enough attention
to talk about it.


The great feature of the deck
sion, however, is that it shuts the
Standard Oils main loophOle for
escaping the $29,000,000 fine i'm
posed under the same law by Judge
Lands.
